channel.title: Japanese on Gourmand Syndrome
channel.description: Recent content in Japanese on Gourmand Syndrome

item.title: Katsu Hama
item.description: Summary Katsu is some of my favorite Japanese food. We decided to try this restaurant because it was close to where we were in Midtown. This type of food generally is not overly expensive like other Japanese foods, and it’s pretty filling too. And for whatever reason, the pork cutlets are always really good and hearty too. The Berkshire pork cutlet is basically a slightly fattier piece compared to a regular pork cutlet.

item.title: Nonono
item.description: Summary The people behind My Name is Han opened a cool Yakitori place. On a Saturday, it was easy to get a table for 2. The zuke maguro is marinated tuna sashimi with fresh jalapeno sauce. The tuna was actually pretty good, and the jalapeno sauce has a nice kick to it. For the yakitoris: - The skirt with shishito was pretty good. There wasn’t anything super special about it, but I really liked the flavor of the coal they use here.

item.title: Kondo
item.description: Summary This is a very clean sushi spot close to the Broadway subway station off the yellow N and W line. Instead of going all sushi, I decided to mix it up a little with standard Japanese snacks over straight up sushi cut rolls, sorta. The sea snails were cooked in a dashi broth, and were pretty tasty. I think my piece wasn’t cleaned entirely properly because there was some grit, but it tasted ok.

item.title: Shiro
item.description: Summary This restaurant is out in the Pasadena area and is suppose to be pretty decent. After going here, I am convinced that many people just have bad taste in food. The clams were decent. Not great, but not bad. I can’t really describe this because it tastes exactly how it looks. If you think good, then it’s good. And if you think bad, then yeah it probably tasted bad.

item.title: Hirohisa
item.description: Summary Hirohisa apparently has been around for a while, but they just received a Michelin star again this year. With that in mind, I felt the price point $160 pp for the 9-course tasting + $70 sake pairing was a decent price for splurging for a special someone’s birthday dinner. Just a note: they do respect some allergy restrictions. My guest had lobster specific allergies, and they were able to accomodate easy.

item.title: Ninja
item.description: Food was bland, but the steak was surprisingly good for a touristy spot. There are a lot of scares from the ninja waiters. The sushi itself was also pretty bland and mediocre.

item.title: Dokodemo
item.description: Decent Japanese snack place with okonomiyaki and octopus balls. But that’s about it.

item.title: Tonkatsu Matsunoya
item.description: This place is pretty bomb and NO TIP NEEDED! Would come back here again. The crust was really good.

item.title: Mi Ne Sushi
item.description: Just ok and also cheap sushi. Not great. Don’t trust people when they say this is good sushi.

item.title: Momoya
item.description: Just ok. Cheap sushi. Don’t trust people’s palettes when they say this place is good.

item.title: Kazunori
item.description: Spawned off LA’s Sugarfish. Cheap 6 piece hand rolls. $32 includes tax, tip, and a Mexican coke. Still room for dessert unfortunately.

item.title: Tsurutontan
item.description: Pretty decent udon. Got the kimchi jigae variant. A bit sweet and would try something else. Came back here another time, and the basic variant was probably better than everything else. Some of the fancier udon variants are just not that great.

item.title: Sushi on Jones
item.description: Another omakase. Decent sushi place. Fast in/out in 20 minutes. $58 total. Got an extra toro/uni hand roll which added another $20ish.

item.title: Ootoya
item.description: Pretty good bento boxing place. A little pricey but no need to tip.

item.title: Nakazawa
item.description: It’s an Omakase. Michelin 1 star. Very good for the price. Got sake pairing + sushi + EXTRAS… only $220 per person. The lychee sorbet was awesome.

item.title: Shuko
item.description: Really good. Got the kaiseki ($175), and they gave a lot of sushi pieces. The dessert, rhubarb sorbet, was really good.

item.title: Shabu Tatsu
item.description: Fairly inexpensive, but not very OG. Portions are small, and no where as good as LA shabu shabu.

item.title: Robataya Ny
item.description: This place was such a gimmick. Very showy like Benihanas but way worse. Food was just ok and super pricey. They have bday celebrations… super weird. But it’s CLOSED.

item.title: Kosaka
item.description: Pretty good kaiseki (~$180).

item.title: Mew Men
item.description: Japanese lunch was cheap and good. But this place is CLOSED now replaced by Menkoi Sato, a ramen place.

item.title: Sake Bar Hagi
item.description: Decent yakitori skewers, but that’s about it. A bit pricey and always a line.

item.title: Abiko Curry
item.description: One of my top favorite curry places. Warning: the medium is actually pretty spicy.

item.title: Curry Ya
item.description: The spicy pork tonkatsu was pretty good. One of the better Japanese curry places in the city.

item.title: Uma Temakeria
item.description: Terrible “sushi burrito” This place is CLOSED for obvious reasons.

item.title: Go Go Curry
item.description: Good lunch spot and fast. Cheap.
